name: airprowl
summary: Real-time wireless scanner for Linux. Tracks nearby Bluetooth, WiFi (APs + clients), and Sub-GHz (315/433/868/915 MHz) devices with live RSSI, EMA-smoothed averages, trend arrows, distance estimates, vendor identification, and persistent custom names. Runs as an interactive TUI or as a headless CLI that emits JSON.

documentation.readme: Real-time wireless scanner for Linux. Tracks nearby Bluetooth, WiFi (APs + clients), and Sub-GHz (315/433/868/915 MHz) devices with live RSSI, EMA-smoothed averages, trend arrows, distance estimates, vendor identification, and persistent custom names. Runs as an interactive TUI or as a headless CLI that emits JSON.
usage_sections: Tri-band scanning — Bluetooth, WiFi, and Sub-GHz ISM bands, one tool
Bluetooth (BR/EDR + BLE) — direct BlueZ D-Bus, queries addresstype, manufacturerdata, icon
WiFi APs — periodic nmcli scans (no root needed)
WiFi clients — virtual monitor interface + 802.11 frame parsing (probe requests, data, QoS) for passive sniffing
Sub-GHz — direct USB control of a Yard Stick One (CC1111) across 315/433/868/915 MHz

arguments.name: -contained
arguments.description: OUI + Bluetooth SIG databases are embedded).

arguments.name: -GHz
arguments.description: frequency in Hz as decimal string (e.g. `433920000`)

arguments.name: --duration
arguments.description: Total run duration in seconds.

arguments.name: --scan
arguments.description: Perform a scan only (do not exploit).

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

examples: sudo apt install network-manager iw bluez libusb-1.0-0 libdbus-1-dev pkg-config
sudo pacman -S networkmanager iw bluez libusb pkg-config
sudo ./target/release/airprowl         # adds client sniffing + Yard Stick
sudo airprowl --scan wifi --duration 15    # WiFi w/ monitor-mode client sniffing

Tracks nearby Bluetooth, WiFi (APs + clients), and Sub-GHz (315/433/868/915 MHz) devices with live RSSI, EMA-smoothed averages, trend arrows, distance estimates, vendor identification, and persistent custom names. documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/bernisnukic/airprowl.git. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `sudo apt install network-manager iw bluez libusb-1.0-0 libdbus-1-dev pkg-config`. Further invocations from upstream: `sudo pacman -S networkmanager iw bluez libusb pkg-config`; `cargo build --release`. Real-time wireless scanner for Linux. Tracks nearby Bluetooth, WiFi (APs + clients), and Sub-GHz (315/433/868/915 MHz) devices with live RSSI, EMA-smoothed averages, trend arrows, distance estimates, vendor identification, and persistent custom names. Runs as an interactive TUI or as a headless CLI that emits JSON.
